Six persons K, L, M, N, O, and P are sitting around a hexagonal table. K is opposite to N. L is between K and M. P is to the immediate left of N. Who is between N and O?

Seven people A, B, C, D, E, F, and G are sitting in a row facing North. B is second to the left of D. E is third to the left of G. C is sitting in the middle. F is to the immediate right of C. A is at one of the ends. Who is sitting at the extreme right end?

In a circular arrangement of 6 people, A is facing B. B is to the right of E and left of C. C is to the left of D. F is to the right of A. Now D exchanges his seat with F and E with B. Who will be to the left of D?

Eight people are sitting in two parallel rows facing each other. Row 1 (A, B, C, D) faces South and Row 2 (P, Q, R, S) faces North. C is opposite to S. B is to the immediate left of C. Q is opposite to B. R is between P and Q. Who is opposite to P?
Six people are sitting in a circle. J is between L and O. N is between K and M. L is to the immediate left of K. Who is opposite to O?

Seven students P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V are standing in a row. Q is to the left of R but to the right of P. O is to the right of S but to the left of T. R is to the left of S. U is between Q and P. Who is in the middle?

Five girls are sitting in a circle facing the center. Rashi is to the left of Pooja. Arpita is between Pooja and Kavita. If Rashi and Pooja exchange their seats, who will be to the immediate right of Rashi?

Six friends P, Q, R, S, T, and U are sitting around a triangular table, two on each side. P and Q are on the same side. R is opposite to P. S is to the immediate left of R. T is on the same side as R. Who is to the immediate right of Q?

A group of eight people are sitting in a row. Three people are between A and B. C is to the immediate right of A. D is in the middle of the row. If B is at the extreme left end, what is the position of C from the left?

Eight people A through H sit around a square table. Four sit at corners facing the center; four sit at mid-sides facing away. G sits at a corner. C is second to the right of G. H is third to the left of C. D sits at a mid-side and is not adjacent to C or G. B is second to the right of E. F is not a neighbor of G. Who faces the center?

Ten people sit in two parallel rows. Row 1: P, Q, R, S, T (facing South). Row 2: A, B, C, D, E (facing North). R is opposite to the one who is second to the left of B. Q is at an extreme end. Only two people sit between Q and T. B is to the immediate right of E. E is opposite to P. S is to the left of R. Who is opposite to D?

Seven people P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V sit in a row. Some face North, some South. P sits second from the left end. U sits third to the right of P. V sits to the immediate left of U. V faces North. T sits second to the right of V. R and Q face the same direction as P. Neighbors of U face opposite directions. If T faces North, who sits at the extreme right end?

Eight persons M, N, O, P, Q, R, S, and T sit around a circle. Three face away from the center. S is second to the left of N. R is third to the right of N. O is second to the left of R. M is second to the right of O. T is to the immediate right of M. Neighbors of T face away from the center. Q is not a neighbor of S. Who is third to the left of M?

Twelve people sit in two rows. Row 1: A to F (South). Row 2: P to U (North). C is third to the left of B. Either C or B is at an end. U is opposite to B. S is second to the left of U. R is opposite to E, who is not a neighbor of B. D is second to the left of A. P is not at an end. T is to the right of Q. Who is opposite to Q?

Eight people J, K, L, M, N, O, P, and Q sit around a circular table. Some face center, some face out. O is third to the right of L. Both face center. J is second to the left of O. Q is second to the left of J. Q and J face same direction. P is third to the left of Q. K is second to the right of N. N faces out. M is not a neighbor of L. How many face out?

Nine people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, and I are sitting in a row facing North. C is third to the left of G. B is at one of the ends. Only one person between B and I. E is fourth to the right of I. H is third to the right of D. F is between G and E. Who is in the exact middle of the row?

Eight people sit in a circle. Four are Engineers and four are Doctors. No two Engineers sit together. E1 is opposite D1. E2 is to the right of D1. D2 is opposite E2. E3 is second to the left of E2. D3 is between E1 and E3. Where is D4?
A, B, C, D, E, F, G are sitting in a row. A and G are at ends. B is second to the left of G. E is between A and C. There are more than three people between A and F. What is the position of D?

Six family members sit around a circle. Grandmother is opposite Father. Son is to the right of Father. Mother is between Son and Grandmother. Daughter is to the left of Father. Grandfather is the remaining person. If Daughter and Mother exchange seats, who is to the left of Grandmother?

If South-East becomes North, North-East becomes West and so on. What will West become?
A man walks 2 km North, then turns East and walks 10 km. After this he turns North and walks 3 km. Again he turns East and walks 2 km. How far is he from the starting point?

A person faces North. He turns 45° in the clockwise direction and then 90° in the anticlockwise direction. Which direction is he facing now?

I am facing East. I turn 100° in the clockwise direction and then 145° in the anticlockwise direction. Which direction am I facing now?
A man is facing North-West. He turns 90° in the clockwise direction, then 180° in the anticlockwise direction and then another 90° in the same direction. Which direction is he facing now?

Starting from a point P, Sachin walked 20 m towards South. He turned left and walked 30 m. He then turned left and walked 20 m. He again turned left and walked 40 m and reached a point Q. How far and in which direction is the point Q from the point P?

One morning after sunrise, Vikram and Shailesh were standing in a lawn with their backs towards each other. Vikram's shadow fell exactly towards his left-hand side. Which direction was Shailesh facing?

A rat runs 20' towards East and turns to right, runs 10' and turns to right, runs 9' and again turns to left, runs 5' and then turns to left, runs 12' and finally turns to left and runs 6'. Now, which direction is the rat facing?

A man starts from his house and walks 10 km North, then turns Left and walks 6 km, then turns Right and walks 7 km. He finally turns Right and walks 6 km. How far is he from his starting point?

First described by Garrett Hardin, this concept is often applied to environmental issues like overfishing and climate change. It highlights the failure of individual rationality to produce a collectively optimal outcome, suggesting that without regulation or private ownership, shared resources are doomed to collapse. While this allows for recovery from brain injuries, it also means that repetitive negative behaviors can "wire" the brain in detrimental ways.
